methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series C → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.7x) to $14.0B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in AI & Software. Range: $3.2B - $39.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in AI & Compute

private.name: Figure AI
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series C), post-money $39B

data.narrative:
Skild AI is a Series C artificial intelligence and software company founded by Deepak Pathak and Abhinav Gupta, currently valued at $14.0B+ post-money after raising $1.4B+. The valuation analysis draws on a comp universe of nine private peers—including Figure AI ($39.0B), Wayve ($8.6B), Physical Intelligence ($5.6B), and Black Forest Labs ($3.2B)—and one public comparable (Palantir, ticker PLTR) in the AI and software sector.

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis, using the most recent $14.0B post-money as a base and applying a typical 1.7x step-up multiplier from Series C to the next stage, implies a valuation of $23.8B. Peer-median analysis across four same-sector and same/adjacent-stage comparables yields a median valuation of $7.1B, with a range spanning $3.2B to $39.0B. The wide peer range reflects heterogeneity in business models and progress within the AI subsector.

Triangulating these two methods produces a valuation range of $6.0B (low) to $27.4B (high), with a median of $15.4B. The current $14.0B valuation sits near the midpoint of this range. Key uncertainties include the relative applicability of the 1.7x stage-progression multiplier to frontier AI, given recent market volatility, and whether Skild AI's technology and commercialization progress more closely tracks the lower-median peer cohort or outliers such as Figure AI.
